frameworks.name: EU AI Act
frameworks.full_name: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence
frameworks.description: World's first comprehensive AI law. Risk-based approach classifying AI systems into unacceptable, high-risk, limited, and minimal risk categories.

key_requirements.description: AI systems in Annex III areas (biometrics, critical infrastructure, education, employment, etc.) require conformity assessments
Continuous iterative process throughout AI system lifecycle
High-risk AI systems must be designed to enable human oversight
Built to be effectively overseen by natural persons

examples: Real-time biometric ID in public spaces

penalties.description: Up to €35M or 7% of global annual turnover

frameworks.name: UNESCO Recommendation on AI Ethics
frameworks.full_name: Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
frameworks.description: First global normative instrument on AI ethics. Non-binding recommendation providing ethical framework for AI development and deployment.

key_requirements.description: AI systems must respect, protect and promote human rights and fundamental freedoms
AI actors should commit to ensuring transparency and explainability of AI systems
Member States should ensure that AI systems do not displace ultimate human responsibility
AI should be assessed in terms of environmental and ecosystem impact

frameworks.name: OECD AI Principles
frameworks.description: First intergovernmental standard on AI. Adopted by 46 countries and serves as basis for G20 AI Principles.

key_requirements.description: AI should benefit people and the planet by driving inclusive growth, sustainable development and well-being
AI systems should be designed to respect rule of law, human rights, democratic values
Transparency and responsible disclosure around AI systems
AI systems should be robust, secure and safe throughout their lifecycle
Organisations and individuals developing AI should be accountable

recommendations_for_governments: Invest in AI research and development
Foster a digital ecosystem for AI
Build human capacity and preparing for labour market transformation

frameworks.name: African Union AI Strategy
frameworks.description: Continental strategy for responsible AI development in Africa, emphasizing local context and development priorities.

key_requirements.description: Building AI skills and research capacity across the continent
Developing computing and data infrastructure
Establishing regulatory frameworks adapted to African context
Supporting AI innovation ecosystems and startups

frameworks.name: ASEAN Guide on AI Governance
frameworks.full_name: ASEAN Guide on AI Governance and Ethics
frameworks.description: Non-binding guidelines for responsible AI development in Southeast Asia, balancing innovation with risk management.

key_requirements.description: AI systems should be transparent and explainable
AI should not discriminate or cause unfair bias
AI systems should be secure and safe
AI should augment human capabilities
AI should respect data protection principles
Clear accountability for AI outcomes
AI systems should work as intended
